title: Profiling, Allocator Performance, and Hardening · Object-Lifetime Lens: Allocation Telemetry
summary: A low-latency gateway reduces allocations but increases peak memory, fragmentation, and tail pauses under changed traffic. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem allocation telemetry increment in measured allocator optimization report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Measure allocation rate, size distribution, lifetime, peak live bytes, failures, and call sites with bounded telemetry.
Maintain a defect-by-tool and platform matrix so unsupported configurations and blind spots remain explicit.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Allocation Telemetry · Theory Allocation Telemetry
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 2435. Produce bounded C evidence to compare live, reserved, and peak memory before optimizing; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure allocation rate, size distribution, lifetime, peak live bytes, failures, and call sites with bounded telemetry.

steps.title: Build Allocation Telemetry evidence · Theory Allocation Telemetry
steps.instruction:
For this theory demonstration of cmem-allocation-telemetry, write the complete compile-ready C program headed "/* Evidence: cmem-allocation-telemetry-19xsca */" for changed case 9150. It must compare live, reserved, and peak memory before optimizing, reject one changed boundary, print an observable result and invariant, and return nonzero when the invariant fails. Keep instant code within the PicoC 3.2.2 practice subset; do not claim C23, optimizing compiler, sanitizer, ABI, thread, operating-system, FFI, load, or production evidence until its explicit controlled transfer gate passes.
